pages.raw_text:
Practically the whole Northern
Pacific and Great Northern systems
are now open for traffic. Trains
will soon be running on the old
schedule again on the G. N. and N.
p. trains are today about five
hours late on the average. Heavy
freights are being fastened both
ways.


The o. R. & N. line was not open
between Pendleton and Walla
Walla today. but all the main line
is clear and delayed freight is be-
ing fastened to its destination as
fast as possible. About 260 cars
of freight are standing on the
tracks at Umatilla, waiting to be
distributed. Much OF the freight
on the tracks On all the roads is
consigned TO Spokane.


Stores are running short all over
town. No spring goods are on
hand and supplies in many of the
grocery lines are badly depleted.
Prices are advancing On account of
the delay.
